metadata.name: mistletoe
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A fast, extensible Markdown parser in pure Python.

functions.name: mistletoe.utils.traverse
documentation:
Traverse the syntax tree, recursively yielding children.

Args:

> source: The source syntax token
> klass: filter children by a certain token class
> depth (int): The depth to recurse into the tree
> include_source (bool): whether to first yield the source element
>
> > (provided it passes any given `klass` filter)

Yields:

: A container for an element, its parent and depth

modules.name: mistletoe.html_renderer
documentation: HTML renderer for mistletoe.

variables.name: mistletoe.html_renderer.HTMLRenderer
documentation: Deprecated name of the `HtmlRenderer` class.

classes.name: mistletoe.html_renderer.HtmlRenderer

methods.name: mistletoe.html_renderer.HtmlRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args: : extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens. html_escape_double_quotes (bool): whether to also escape double > quotes when HTML-escaping rendered text. html_escape_single_quotes (bool): whether to also escape single : quotes when HTML-escaping rendered text. process_html_tokens (bool): whether to include HTML tokens in the : processing. If `False`, HTML markup will be treated as plain text: e.g. input `
` will be rendered as `<br>`. kwargs: additional parameters to be passed to the ancestor's : constructor.

methods.name: mistletoe.html_renderer.HtmlRenderer.escape_html_text
documentation:
Like `html.escape()`, but this  looks into the current rendering options
to decide which of the quotes (double, single, or both) to escape.

Intended for escaping text content. To escape content of an attribute,
simply call `html.escape()`.

methods.name: mistletoe.html_renderer.HtmlRenderer.escape_url
documentation: Escape urls to prevent code injection craziness. (Hopefully.)

HTML renderer class.

See mistletoe.base_renderer module for more info.

modules.name: mistletoe.ast_renderer
documentation: Abstract syntax tree renderer for mistletoe.

functions.name: mistletoe.ast_renderer.get_ast
documentation:
Recursively unrolls token attributes into dictionaries (token.children
into lists).

Returns:
    a dictionary of token's attributes.

variables.name: mistletoe.ast_renderer.ASTRenderer
documentation: Deprecated name of the `AstRenderer` class.

methods.name: mistletoe.ast_renderer.AstRenderer.render
documentation:
Returns the string representation of the AST.

Overrides super().render. Delegates the logic to get_ast.

modules.name: mistletoe.span_tokenizer
documentation: Inline tokenizer for mistletoe.

modules.name: mistletoe
documentation: Make mistletoe easier to import.

functions.name: mistletoe.markdown
documentation:
Converts markdown input to the output supported by the given renderer.
If no renderer is supplied, `HtmlRenderer` is used.

Note that extra token types supported by the given renderer
are automatically (and temporarily) added to the parsing process.

modules.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer
documentation: Markdown renderer for mistletoe.

classes.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.BlankLine
documentation:
Blank line token. Represents a single blank line.
This is a leaf block token without children.

classes.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.LinkReferenceDefinition
documentation:
Link reference definition. ([label]: dest "title")

Not included in the parsing process, but called by `LinkReferenceDefinitionBlock`.

Attributes:
    label (str): link label, used in link references.
    dest (str): link target.
    title (str): link title (default to empty).

classes.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.LinkReferenceDefinitionBlock
documentation:
A sequence of link reference definitions.
This is a leaf block token. Its children are link reference definition tokens.

This class inherits from `Footnote` and modifies the behavior of the constructor,
to keep the tokens in the AST.

classes.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.Fragment
documentation:
Markdown fragment. Used when rendering trees of span tokens into flat sequences.
May carry additional data in addition to the text.

Attributes:
    text (str): markdown fragment.

classes.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens.
    max_line_length (int): if specified, the document is word wrapped to the
        specified line length when rendered. Otherwise the formatting from the
        original (parsed) document is retained as much as possible.
    normalize_whitespace (bool): if `False`, the renderer will try to preserve
        as much whitespace as it currently can. For example, you can
        use this flag to control whether to replace the original
        spacing after every list item leader with just 1 space.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.render
documentation: Renders the tree of tokens rooted at the given token into markdown.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.render_raw_text
documentation: rendering of span/inline tokens.
rendered into sequences of Fragments.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.render_document
documentation: rendering of block tokens.
rendered into sequences of lines (strings), to be joined by newlines.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.embed_span
documentation:
Makes fragments from `tokens` and embeds within a leader and a trailer.
The trailer defaults to the same as the leader.
helper methods

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.blocks_to_lines
documentation: Renders a sequence of block tokens into a sequence of lines.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.span_to_lines
documentation: Renders a sequence of span (inline) tokens into a sequence of lines.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.make_fragments
documentation: Renders a sequence of span (inline) tokens into a sequence of Fragments.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.fragments_to_lines
documentation:
Renders a sequence of Fragments into lines.
With word wrapping, if a `max_line_length` is given, or else following the
original text flow as closely as possible.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.make_words
documentation:
Aggregates and splits a sequence of Fragments into words, i.e., strings
        which do not contain breakable spaces or line breaks. The exception is
        hard line breaks, which are represented by the string `
`.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.prefix_lines
documentation:
Prepends a prefix string to a sequence of lines. The first line may
have a different prefix from the following lines.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.table_row_to_text
documentation: Renders each table cell on a table row to text. No word wrapping.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.calculate_table_column_widths
documentation: Calculates column widths for a table.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.table_separator_line_to_text
documentation:
Creates the text for the line separating header from contents in a table
given column widths and alignments.

Note: uses dashes for left justified columns, not a colon followed by dashes.

methods.name: mistletoe.markdown_renderer.MarkdownRenderer.table_row_to_line
documentation: Pads/aligns the text for a table row and add the borders (pipe characters).

Markdown renderer.

Designed to make as "clean" a roundtrip as possible, markdown -> parsing -> rendering -> markdown,
except for nonessential whitespace. Except when rendering with word wrapping enabled.

Includes `HtmlBlock` and `HtmlSpan` tokens in the parsing.

modules.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer

Table of contents support for mistletoe.

See `if __name__ == '__main__'` section for sample usage.

variables.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TOCRenderer
documentation: Deprecated name of the `TocRenderer` class.

classes.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TocRenderer

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TocRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens.
    depth (int): the maximum level of heading to be included in TOC.
    omit_title (bool): whether to ignore tokens where token.level == 1.
    filter_conds (list): when any of these functions evaluate to true,
                        current heading will not be included.
    **kwargs: additional parameters to be passed to the ancestor's
              constructor.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TocRenderer.toc
documentation: Returns table of contents as a block_token.List instance.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TocRenderer.render_heading
documentation:
Overrides super().render_heading; stores rendered heading first,
then returns it.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.toc_renderer.TocRenderer.parse_rendered_heading
documentation: Helper method; converts rendered heading to plain text.
Extends HtmlRenderer class for table of contents support.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.xwiki20_renderer.XWiki20Renderer.__init__

modules.name: mistletoe.block_token
documentation: Built-in block-level token classes.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_token.tokenize
documentation:
A wrapper around block_tokenizer.tokenize. Pass in all block-level
token constructors as arguments to block_tokenizer.tokenize.

Doing so (instead of importing block_token module in block_tokenizer)
avoids cyclic dependency issues, and allows for future injections of
custom token classes.

_token_types variable is at the bottom of this module.

See also: block_tokenizer.tokenize, span_token.tokenize_inner.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_token.add_token
documentation:
Allows external manipulation of the parsing process.
This function is usually called in BaseRenderer.__enter__.

Arguments:
    token_cls (SpanToken): token to be included in the parsing process.
    position (int): the position for the token class to be inserted into.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_token.remove_token
documentation:
Allows external manipulation of the parsing process.
This function is usually called in BaseRenderer.__exit__.

Arguments:
    token_cls (BlockToken): token to be removed from the parsing process.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_token.reset_tokens
documentation: Resets global _token_types to all token classes in __all__.

variables.name: mistletoe.block_token.HTMLBlock
documentation: Deprecated name of the `HtmlBlock` class.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.BlockToken
documentation:
Base class for block-level tokens. Recursively parse inner tokens.

Naming conventions:

    * lines denotes a list of (possibly unparsed) input lines, and is
      commonly used as the argument name for constructors.

    * BlockToken.children is a list with all the inner tokens (thus if
      a token has children attribute, it is not a leaf node; if a token
      calls span_token.tokenize_inner, it is the boundary between
      span-level tokens and block-level tokens);

    * BlockToken.start takes a line from the document as argument, and
      returns a boolean representing whether that line marks the start
      of the current token. Every subclass of BlockToken must define a
      start function (see block_tokenizer.tokenize).

    * BlockToken.read takes the rest of the lines in the document as an
      iterator (including the start line), and consumes all the lines
      that should be read into this token.

      Default to stop at an empty line.

      Note that BlockToken.read does not have to return a list of lines.
      Because the return value of this function will be directly
      passed into the token constructor, we can return any relevant
      parsing information, sometimes even ready-made tokens,
      into the constructor. See block_tokenizer.tokenize.

      If BlockToken.read returns None, the read result is ignored,
      but the token class is responsible for resetting the iterator
      to a previous state. See block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.get_pos,
      block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.set_pos.

Attributes:
    children (list): inner tokens.
    line_number (int): starting line (1-based).

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Document
documentation:
Document token.
This is a container block token. Its children are block tokens - container or leaf ones.

Attributes:
    footnotes (dictionary): link reference definitions.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Heading
documentation:
ATX heading token. (["### some heading ###\n"])
This is a leaf block token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.

Attributes:
    level (int): heading level.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.SetextHeading
documentation:
Setext heading token.
This is a leaf block token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.

Not included in the parsing process, but called by Paragraph.__new__.

Attributes:
    level (int): heading level.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Quote
documentation:
Block quote token. (["> # heading\n", "> paragraph\n"])
This is a container block token. Its children are block tokens - container or leaf ones.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Paragraph

class_variables.name: mistletoe.block_token.Paragraph.parse_setext
documentation: can be disabled by Quote

Paragraph token. (["some
", "continuous
", "lines
"])
This is a leaf block token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.BlockCode

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.BlockCode.content
documentation: Returns the code block content.

Indented code block token.
This is a leaf block token with a single child of type span_token.RawText.

Attributes:
    language (str): always the empty string.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.CodeFence

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.CodeFence.content
documentation: Returns the code block content.

Fenced code block token. (["```sh\n", "rm -rf /", ..., "```"])
This is a leaf block token with a single child of type span_token.RawText.

Attributes:
    language (str): language of code block (default to empty).

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.List
documentation:
List token.
This is a container block token. Its children are ListItem tokens.

Attributes:
    loose (bool): whether the list is loose.
    start (NoneType or int): None if unordered, starting number if ordered.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.ListItem

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.ListItem.parse_continuation
documentation:
Returns content (i.e. the line with the prepend stripped off) iff the line
is a valid continuation line for a list item with the given prepend length,
otherwise None.

Note that the list item may still continue even if this test doesn't pass
due to lazy continuation.

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.ListItem.parse_marker
documentation:
Returns a tuple (prepend, leader, content) iff the line has a valid leader and at
least one space separating leader and content, or if the content is empty, in which
case there need not be any spaces.
The return value is None if the line doesn't have a valid marker.

The leader is a bullet list marker, or an ordered list marker.

The indentation is spaces before the leader.

The prepend is the start position of the content, i.e., the indentation required
for continuation lines.

List item token.
This is a container block token. Its children are block tokens - container or leaf ones.

Not included in the parsing process, but called by List.

Attributes:
    leader (string): a bullet list marker or an ordered list marker.
    indentation (int): spaces before the leader.
    prepend (int): the start position of the content, i.e., the indentation required
                   for continuation lines.
    loose (bool): whether the list is loose.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Table

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.Table.split_delimiter
documentation:
Helper function; returns a list of align options.

        Args:
            delimiter (str): e.g.: "| :--- | :---: | ---: |
"

        Returns:
            a list of align options (None, 0 or 1).

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.Table.parse_align

documentation:
Table token. See its GFM definition at <https://github.github.com/gfm/#tables-extension->.
This is a container block token. Its children are TableRow tokens.

Class attributes:
    interrupt_paragraph: indicates whether tables should interrupt paragraphs
    during parsing. The default is true.

Attributes:
    header: header row (TableRow).
    column_align (list): align options for each column (default to [None]).

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.TableRow
documentation:
Table row token. Supports escaped pipes in table cells (for primary use within code spans).
This is a container block token. Its children are TableCell tokens.

Should only be called by Table.__init__().

Attributes:
    row_align (list): align options for each column (default to [None]).

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.TableCell
documentation:
Table cell token.
This is a leaf block token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.

Should only be called by TableRow.__init__().

Attributes:
    align (bool): align option for current cell (default to None).

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.Footnote

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.Footnote.match_link_label
documentation: Matches: up to three spaces, "[", label, "]".

Footnote token. One or more "link reference definitions" according to the CommonMark spec.
This is a leaf block token without children.

The constructor returns None, because available footnote definitions are parsed
and stored into the root node within `Footnote.read()`. We don't put instances of
this class into the resulting AST.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.ThematicBreak
documentation:
Thematic break token (a.k.a. horizontal rule.)
This is a leaf block token without children.

classes.name: mistletoe.block_token.HtmlBlock

methods.name: mistletoe.block_token.HtmlBlock.content
documentation: Returns the raw HTML content.

Block-level HTML token.
This is a leaf block token with a single child of type span_token.RawText,
which holds the raw HTML content.

modules.name: mistletoe.contrib.mathjax
documentation: Provides MathJax support for rendering Markdown with LaTeX to html.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.mathjax.MathJaxRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    **kwargs: additional parameters to be passed to the ancestors'
              constructors.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.mathjax.MathJaxRenderer.render_math
documentation:
Convert single dollar sign enclosed math expressions to the `\(...\)` syntax, to support
the default MathJax settings which ignore single dollar signs as described at
<https://docs.mathjax.org/en/latest/basic/mathematics.html#tex-and-latex-input>.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.mathjax.MathJaxRenderer.render_document
documentation: Append CDN link for MathJax to the end of <body>.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.pygments_renderer.PygmentsRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens.
    style (str): short name of the style to be used by Pygments' `HtmlFormatter`,
              see `pygments.styles.get_style_by_name()`.
    fail_on_unsupported_language (bool): whether to let Pygments' `ClassNotFound`
              be thrown when there is an unsupported language found on
              a code block.
              If `False`, then language is guessed instead of throwing the error.
    **kwargs: additional parameters to be passed to the ancestor's
              constructor.

functions.name: mistletoe.core_tokens.get_link_label
documentation:
Normalize and look up `text` among the footnotes.
Returns (destination, title) if successful, otherwise None.

variables.name: mistletoe.contrib.md2jira.helpString
documentation:
Command-line utility to convert Markdown (CommonMark) to JIRA markup.

JIRA markup spec: https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/WikiRendererHelpAction.jspa?section=all
CommonMark spec: http://spec.commonmark.org/0.30/#introduction

variables.name: mistletoe.contrib.md2jira.MarkdownToJIRA
documentation: Deprecated name of the `MarkdownToJira` class.

modules.name: mistletoe.base_renderer
documentation: Base class for renderers.

classes.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer.render
documentation:
Grabs the class name from input token and finds its corresponding
render function.

Basically a janky way to do polymorphism.

Arguments:
    token: whose __class__.__name__ is in self.render_map.

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer.render_inner
documentation:
Recursively renders child tokens. Joins the rendered
strings with no space in between.

If newlines / spaces are needed between tokens, add them
in their respective templates, or override this function
in the renderer subclass, so that whitespace won't seem to
appear magically for anyone reading your program.

Arguments:
    token: a branch node who has children attribute.

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer.__enter__
documentation: Make renderer classes into context managers.

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer.__exit__
documentation:
Make renderer classes into context managers.

Reset block_token._token_types and span_token._token_types.

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer._tokens_from_module
documentation:
Helper method; takes a module and returns a list of all token classes
specified in module.__all__. Useful when custom tokens are defined in a
separate module.

methods.name: mistletoe.base_renderer.BaseRenderer.render_raw_text
documentation: Default render method for RawText. Simply return token.content.

Base class for renderers.

All renderers should ...
*   ... define all render functions specified in self.render_map;
*   ... be a context manager (by inheriting __enter__ and __exit__);

Custom renderers could ...
*   ... add additional tokens into the parsing process by passing custom
    tokens to super().__init__();
*   ... add additional render functions by appending to self.render_map;

Usage:
    Suppose SomeRenderer inherits BaseRenderer, and fin is the input file.
    The syntax looks something like this:

        >>> from mistletoe import Document
        >>> from some_renderer import SomeRenderer
        >>> with SomeRenderer() as renderer:
        ...     rendered = renderer.render(Document(fin))

    See mistletoe.html_renderer for an implementation example.

Naming conventions:
    *   The keys of self.render_map should exactly match the class
        name of tokens;
    *   Render function names should be of form: "render_" + the
        "snake-case" form of token's class name.

Attributes:
    render_map (dict): maps tokens to their corresponding render functions.
    _extras (list): a list of custom tokens to be added to the
                    parsing process.

variables.name: mistletoe.contrib.jira_renderer.JIRARenderer

classes.name: mistletoe.contrib.jira_renderer.JiraRenderer

Args:
    extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens.

methods.name: mistletoe.contrib.jira_renderer.JiraRenderer._block_eol
documentation:
Jira syntax is very limited when it comes to lists: whenever
we put an empty line anywhere in a list, it gets terminated
and there seems to be no workaround for this. Also to have blocks
like paragraphs really vertically separated, we need to put
an empty line between them. This function handles these two cases.

JIRA renderer class.

See mistletoe.base_renderer module for more info.

functions.name: mistletoe.cli.convert_file
documentation: Parse a Markdown file and dump the output to stdout.

functions.name: mistletoe.cli.interactive
documentation:
Parse user input, dump to stdout, rinse and repeat.
Python REPL style.

modules.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer
documentation: Block-level tokenizer for mistletoe.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Searches for token_types in iterable.

Args:
    iterable (list): user input lines to be parsed.
    token_types (list): a list of block-level token constructors.

Returns:
    block-level token instances.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.tokenize_block
documentation:
Returns a list of tuples (token_type, read_result, line_number).

Footnotes are parsed here, but span-level parsing has not
started yet.

functions.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.make_tokens
documentation:
Takes a list of tuples (token_type, read_result, line_number),
applies token_type(read_result), and sets the line_number attribute.

Footnotes are already parsed before this point,
and span-level parsing is started here.

methods.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.get_pos
documentation:
Returns the current reading position.
The result is an opaque value which can be passed to `set_pos`.

methods.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.set_pos
documentation: Sets the current reading position.

methods.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.anchor
documentation: @deprecated use `get_pos` instead

methods.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.FileWrapper.reset
documentation: @deprecated use `set_pos` instead

classes.name: mistletoe.block_tokenizer.ParseBuffer
documentation:
A wrapper around builtin list,
so that setattr(list, 'loose') is legal.

modules.name: mistletoe.__main__
documentation: Make mistletoe runnable as a script with default settings.

functions.name: mistletoe.__main__.main
documentation: Entry point.

modules.name: mistletoe.latex_renderer
documentation: LaTeX renderer for mistletoe.

methods.name: mistletoe.latex_renderer.LaTeXRenderer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    extras (list): allows subclasses to add even more custom tokens.
    **kwargs: additional parameters to be passed to the ancestor's
              constructor.

methods.name: mistletoe.latex_renderer.LaTeXRenderer.escape_url
documentation:
Quote unsafe chars in urls & escape as needed for LaTeX's hyperref.

%-escapes all characters that are neither in the unreserved chars
("always safe" as per RFC 2396 or RFC 3986) nor in the chars set
'/#:()*?=%@+,&;'

Subsequently, LaTeX-escapes '%' and '#' for hyperref's \url{} to also
work if used within macros like \multicolumn. if \url{} with urls
containing '%' or '#' is used outside of multicolumn-macros, they work
regardless of whether these characters are escaped, and the result
remains the same (at least for pdflatex from TeX Live 2019).

modules.name: mistletoe.span_token
documentation: Built-in span-level token classes.

functions.name: mistletoe.span_token.tokenize_inner
documentation:
A wrapper around span_tokenizer.tokenize. Pass in all span-level token
constructors as arguments to span_tokenizer.tokenize.

Doing so (instead of importing span_token module in span_tokenizer)
avoids cyclic dependency issues, and allows for future injections of
custom token classes.

_token_types variable is at the bottom of this module.

See also: span_tokenizer.tokenize, block_token.tokenize.

functions.name: mistletoe.span_token.add_token
documentation:
Allows external manipulation of the parsing process.
This function is called in BaseRenderer.__enter__.

Arguments:
    token_cls (SpanToken): token to be included in the parsing process.

functions.name: mistletoe.span_token.remove_token
documentation:
Allows external manipulation of the parsing process.
This function is called in BaseRenderer.__exit__.

Arguments:
    token_cls (SpanToken): token to be removed from the parsing process.

functions.name: mistletoe.span_token.reset_tokens
documentation: Resets global _token_types to all token classes in __all__.

documentation: noqa: E221

variables.name: mistletoe.span_token._open_tag

variables.name: mistletoe.span_token.HTMLSpan
documentation: Deprecated name of the `HtmlSpan` class.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.CoreTokens
documentation:
Represents core tokens (Strong, Emphasis, Image, Link) during the early stage of parsing.
Replaced with objects of the proper classes in the final stage of parsing.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.Strong
documentation:
Strong token. ("**some text**")
This is an inline token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.
One of the core tokens.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.Emphasis
documentation:
Emphasis token. ("*some text*")
This is an inline token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.
One of the core tokens.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.InlineCode
documentation:
Inline code token. ("`some code`")
This is an inline token with a single child of type RawText.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.Strikethrough
documentation:
Strikethrough token. ("~~some text~~")
This is an inline token. Its children are inline (span) tokens.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.Image
documentation:
Image token. ("![alt](src "title")")
This is an inline token. Its children are inline (span) tokens holding the image description.
One of the core tokens.

Attributes:
    src (str): image source.
    title (str): image title (default to empty).
    label (str): link label, for reference links.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.Link
documentation:
Link token. ("[name](target "title")")
This is an inline token. Its children are inline (span) tokens holding the link text.
One of the core tokens.

Attributes:
    target (str): link target.
    title (str): link title (default to empty).
    label (str): link label, for reference links.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.AutoLink
documentation:
Autolink token. ("<http://www.google.com>")
This is an inline token with a single child of type RawText.

Attributes:
    children (list): a single RawText node for the link target.
    target (str): link target.
    mailto (bool): true iff the target looks like an email address, but does not have the "mailto:" prefix.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.EscapeSequence
documentation:
Escape sequence token. ("\\*")
This is an inline token with a single child of type RawText.

Attributes:
    children (iterator): a single RawText node containing the escaped character.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.LineBreak
documentation:
Line break token: hard or soft.
This is an inline token without children.

Attributes:
    soft (bool): true if this is a soft line break.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.RawText
documentation:
Raw text token.
This is an inline token without children.

RawText is the only token that accepts a string for its constructor,
instead of a match object. Also, all recursions should bottom out here.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.HtmlSpan
documentation:
Span-level HTML token.
This is an inline token without children.

Attributes:
    content (str): the raw HTML content.

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.XWikiBlockMacroStart
documentation:
A "block" macro opening tag. ("{{macroName<optionalParams>}}<newLine>")

We want to keep it on a separate line instead of "soft" merging it with the *following* line.
Note: The following XWiki tokens are based on the XWiki Syntax 2.0 (or above; 1.0 was deprecated years ago already).

classes.name: mistletoe.span_token.XWikiBlockMacroEnd
documentation:
A "block" macro closing tag. ("<onlySpacesAllowed>{{/macroName}}")

We want to keep it on a separate line instead of "soft" merging it with the *preceding* line.

functions.name: mistletoe.token._short_repr
documentation: Return a shortened `repr` output of value for use in `__repr__` methods.

classes.name: mistletoe.token.Token

methods.name: mistletoe.token.Token.parent
documentation: Returns the parent token, if there is any.

methods.name: mistletoe.token.Token.children
documentation:
Returns the child (nested) tokens.
Returns `None` if the token is a leaf token.

methods.name: mistletoe.token.Token.children
documentation:
"
Sets new child (nested) tokens.
Passed tokens are iterated and their `parent` property is set to
this token.

Base token class.

`Token` has two subclasses:

- `block_token.BlockToken`, for all block level tokens. A block level token
  is text which occupies the entire horizontal width of the "page" and is
  offset for the surrounding sibling block with line breaks.
- `span_token.SpanToken`, for all span-level (or inline-level) tokens.
  A span-level token appears inside the flow of the text lines without any
  surrounding line break.

Custom `__repr__` methods in subclasses: The default `__repr__`
implementation outputs the number of child tokens (from the attribute
`children`) if applicable, and the `content` attribute if applicable.
If any additional attributes should be included in the `__repr__` output,
this can be specified by setting the class attribute `repr_attributes`
to a tuple containing the attribute names to be output.
